title: Make Angular Control Center use real AgentSessions, PolicySnapshots, ToolCalls, Artifact links and authenticated live events

source_analysis.summary: The Control Center UI and adapter API exist now, but several important parts are still MVP/adapters/mocks: sessions are backed by share-session data and in-memory task links, Angular session cards synthesize worker/model/policy/tool-call data, artifact filtering is local and text-based, SSE uses browser EventSource without auth headers, and the backend event stream polls all tasks/policy decisions repeatedly.

problem_statement.short: The Control Center has a good Angular/API skeleton, but it must be bound to real backend execution state instead of synthesized placeholder state.
details: agent/routes/control_center_api.py currently stores task-session links in process memory via _TASK_SESSION_LINKS, which is lost on restart and cannot represent real AgentSession lifecycle.
agent/routes/control_center_api.py currently stores manual approval events in process memory via _APPROVAL_EVENTS, which is not auditable/persistent enough for security-sensitive approvals.

frontend-angular/src/app/features/control-center/components/control-center-sessions.component.ts maps share sessions into CcAgentSession with hardcoded workerType, model, riskLevel, allowedPaths and empty toolCalls.

frontend-angular/src/app/features/control-center/components/control-center-security-inspector.component.ts cannot accurately reason about cloud boundaries because PolicySnapshot has no explicit cloudAllowed/runtime boundary field in the rendered model.

frontend-angular/src/app/features/control-center/services/control-center-event-stream.service.ts uses native EventSource, which cannot attach custom Authorization headers and may fail with header-token auth.

agent/routes/control_center_api.py implements SSE by polling task_repo.get_all() and policy_decision_repo.get_all() in a loop, which is acceptable as MVP but weak for scaling and per-user/per-project filtering.

frontend-angular/src/app/features/control-center/components/control-center-artifact-viewers.component.ts filters project/task/session by local string matching against id/title instead of real API-backed relations.
The diff viewer is intentionally simple and does not yet parse real unified diffs or align side-by-side hunks correctly.
The CodeCompass route exists only as placeholder and is not yet tied to real context scope selection and preview for agent runs.

goal.primary: Convert the Control Center from a Nimbalyst-like UI skeleton into an Ananta-native control plane for real agent execution state, policy decisions, context scopes, artifacts and verification evidence.
outcomes: Task -> AgentSession -> Worker -> ToolCall -> PolicyDecision -> Artifact -> Verification relationships are persistent and queryable.
Angular displays real worker/model/runtime/policy/tool-call/artifact data from the backend instead of hardcoded defaults.
Narrow approvals are persisted, auditable and attached to exact pending actions/tool calls.
Artifacts can be filtered by project_id, task_id, session_id and type server-side.
SSE/WebSocket live events are authenticated and filtered by user/project where appropriate.
Cloud/local/security boundaries are explicit in both backend contracts and Angular inspector.
Tests prove the contracts and the security behavior instead of only checking that endpoints return items.

non_goals: Do not remove the existing share-session/WebRTC feature; it can coexist with AgentSession but must not be confused with an agent execution session.
Do not make Angular the source of truth for policy decisions.
Do not add broad approvals such as all_actions, all_tools or unrestricted_shell.
Do not allow artifact rendering to execute scripts or unsafe HTML.
Do not block incremental delivery; this todo should be implemented in phases.
design_principles: Backend/hub is authoritative for session state, policy state, tool permissions, context scope and approval validity.
Angular is an inspector and operator UI, not a security boundary.
Every high-risk action must be represented as a concrete ToolCall or PendingAction with a stable id before approval is possible.
Every task completion should be evidence-based: artifacts, diffs, tests and verification reports should be visible.
Real state should replace placeholders gradually, but every placeholder must be easy to identify in code and UI.
Default deny must remain visible: denied tools, denied paths and cloud restrictions are first-class display data.

phases.title: Introduce persistent AgentSession model and remove in-memory task-session linkage
current_code_findings: agent/routes/control_center_api.py uses _TASK_SESSION_LINKS: dict[str, str] = {} for session_id -> task_id.
create_task_session() currently calls get_share_session_service().create_session(...) and then stores _TASK_SESSION_LINKS[sid] = task_id.
list_sessions() and get_task_detail() depend on this in-memory map to relate sessions to tasks.

implementation_tasks.title: Add AgentSessionDB persistence model
implementation_tasks.description: Create a persistent table/model for real agent execution sessions, separate from share-session/WebRTC collaboration sessions.
acceptance_criteria: A new AgentSessionDB or equivalent persisted model exists and is registered in the repository registry.
Task-session relation survives process restart because it is stored in the database, not in _TASK_SESSION_LINKS.
Share-session collaboration records remain separate and are not renamed into agent sessions.
A migration/schema update is included in agent/database.py or the existing schema setup mechanism.
Tests can create a task session, reload it through GET /api/sessions and GET /api/tasks/{taskId}, and still receive task_id from persisted state.

implementation_tasks.title: Replace _TASK_SESSION_LINKS in control_center_api.py
implementation_tasks.description: Remove process-local task-session state and use AgentSessionDB queries instead.
acceptance_criteria: _TASK_SESSION_LINKS is removed from agent/routes/control_center_api.py.
GET /api/sessions?task_id=... filters using persisted session.task_id.
GET /api/tasks/{taskId} returns latest or active AgentSession from persisted data.
POST /api/tasks/{taskId}/sessions creates an AgentSessionDB row instead of only a share session link.
The endpoint response clearly identifies whether a returned session is an agent_execution session or a share/collaboration session.

implementation_tasks.title: Define AgentSession status lifecycle
implementation_tasks.description: Normalize the lifecycle states used by backend and Angular.
acceptance_criteria: Backend rejects or normalizes unknown session statuses.
Angular CcAgentSession status union matches backend statuses.
Cancel endpoint sets status=cancelled and cancelled_at/updated_at persistently.
Failed execution records failure_reason.

phases.title: Persist ToolCalls and PendingActions for real policy/approval flow
current_code_findings: frontend-angular/src/app/features/control-center/components/control-center-sessions.component.ts currently maps toolCalls: [] for every session.
frontend-angular/src/app/features/control-center/components/control-center-policy-approval.component.ts lets the user manually type Action ID and Tool Call ID.
agent/routes/control_center_api.py accepts action_id and tool_call_id but does not verify that the pending action exists.

implementation_tasks.title: Add ToolCallDB / PendingActionDB
implementation_tasks.description: Persist proposed, allowed, denied, running and completed tool calls. Every approval must reference one concrete pending action/tool call.
acceptance_criteria: Tool calls are stored persistently and can be queried by session_id.
Tool call arguments are not blindly exposed if they may contain secrets; store preview/hash separately where needed.
A tool call can be in statuses proposed, require_approval, allowed, denied, running, completed or failed.
GET /api/sessions/{sessionId} includes toolCalls or exposes a separate /api/sessions/{sessionId}/tool-calls endpoint.
Angular ToolCallTimeline displays real backend tool calls instead of an empty array.

implementation_tasks.title: Approval endpoint must validate pending action existence
implementation_tasks.description: POST /api/policy/approve must only approve a real pending action/tool call that belongs to the given session and is still approval-eligible.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/policy/approve returns 404 or 400 when action_id/tool_call_id does not exist.
POST /api/policy/approve returns 409 when the tool call is already completed, denied-final, cancelled or expired.
POST /api/policy/approve returns 403 when the authenticated user is not allowed to approve that action.
POST /api/policy/approve still rejects scope != single_action.
A successful approval updates the ToolCall/PendingAction status and writes an auditable approval record.

implementation_tasks.title: Remove manual free-text approval workflow from Angular
implementation_tasks.description: The approval UI should list actual pending actions instead of asking the operator to manually type ids.
acceptance_criteria: ControlCenterPolicyApprovalComponent renders pending approval rows received from backend.
The approve button is attached to a concrete pending action row.
Action ID and Tool Call ID inputs are removed or hidden behind a development-only diagnostic mode.
Approval payload is generated from backend data, not user-typed ids.
UI shows tool name, target path, risk level, reason and matched policy rules before approval.

phases.title: Persist PolicySnapshot and bind real policy data into Security Inspector
current_code_findings: ControlCenterSessionsComponent currently creates policySnapshot with hardcoded riskLevel='medium', allowedPaths=['/'], deniedPaths=[], allowedTools=[], deniedTools=[], policyVersion='v1'.
ControlCenterSecurityInspectorComponent only receives CcPolicySnapshot input and cannot display cloudAllowed/runtime boundary accurately.
control_center_api.py list_workers() guesses boundary from runtime string only.

implementation_tasks.title: Add PolicySnapshotDB for session-time policy state
implementation_tasks.description: Persist the policy state that was active when an AgentSession was created or evaluated.
acceptance_criteria: Every AgentSession has either a policy_snapshot_id or an embedded policy snapshot response generated by backend.
GET /api/sessions/{sessionId} returns real policySnapshot data from persisted backend state.
Allowed and denied tools come from actual policy/capability evaluation, not hardcoded Angular defaults.
Allowed and denied paths come from actual ContextScope/policy evaluation, not hardcoded ['/'].
cloud_allowed and runtime_boundary are explicit backend fields.

implementation_tasks.title: Update Angular policy model and inspector
implementation_tasks.description: Extend CcPolicySnapshot and Security Inspector to show real cloud/local and context boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: CcPolicySnapshot includes cloudAllowed:boolean and runtimeBoundary:'local-only'|'cloud-allowed'|'remote'|'unknown' or equivalent.
Security Inspector shows cloudAllowed/runtimeBoundary explicitly.
cloudBoundaryWarning only triggers when cloudAllowed is true and sensitive allowed paths exist.
Denied sensitive paths are shown as successfully blocked, not as generic cloud risk.
Unit tests cover sensitive allowed paths vs sensitive denied paths.

implementation_tasks.title: Bind worker/model/runtime to real session data
implementation_tasks.description: Sessions page must show the actual worker/model/runtime selected by backend/hub.
acceptance_criteria: ControlCenterSessionsComponent no longer hardcodes workerType='ananta-worker' for every row.
ControlCenterSessionsComponent no longer hardcodes model='n/a'.
ControlCenterSessionsComponent maps workerId, workerType, model and runtime from backend read model.
If backend lacks a value, UI displays 'unknown' and a diagnostic hint instead of pretending a default is real.

phases.title: Artifact relationships, server-side filters and stronger review flow
current_code_findings: HubControlCenterApiClient.listArtifacts() currently calls `${baseUrl}/artifacts` without project/task/session filters.
ControlCenterArtifactBrowserComponent filters project/task/session locally by checking whether id/title contains the typed value.
GET /api/tasks/{taskId} currently returns artifacts: [] in control_center_api.py.

implementation_tasks.title: Add artifact relationship metadata
implementation_tasks.description: Artifacts must be queryable by project_id, task_id, session_id, tool_call_id and artifact type.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact metadata/model supports project_id, task_id, session_id and tool_call_id fields.
Artifacts created by agent sessions are automatically linked to the current AgentSession and task.
GET /api/tasks/{taskId} returns related artifacts instead of always artifacts: [].
Artifact links are preserved after restart.

implementation_tasks.title: Implement API-side artifact filters
implementation_tasks.description: Add query params for artifact listing so Angular does not rely on local id/title search.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact list endpoint accepts project_id filter.
Artifact list endpoint accepts task_id filter.
Artifact list endpoint accepts session_id filter.
Artifact list endpoint accepts type/media type filter.
Angular ArtifactBrowser passes selected filters to the API instead of only filtering local rows.
Tests prove that artifacts from another task/session are not returned when a filter is set.

implementation_tasks.title: Improve diff viewer correctness
implementation_tasks.description: Replace the naive '+'/'-' split mode with a real unified diff parser or a safer clearly-labeled preview.
acceptance_criteria: Side-by-side diff mode correctly handles file headers, hunks, context lines, additions and deletions.
If no real parser is added yet, side-by-side mode is labeled as experimental and tests cover current limitations.
Large diffs remain scrollable and do not freeze the UI for typical project changes.
Diff viewer still escapes all content and never renders diff content as HTML.

implementation_tasks.title: Review flow must be artifact-first
implementation_tasks.description: Task review should show changed files, diffs, test reports and verification evidence directly.
acceptance_criteria: Task detail page or review page shows related diff artifacts.
Verification panel links to test report artifacts through real artifact ids.
A task cannot be marked verified in UI unless backend verification status allows it.
Review page distinguishes generated files, modified files, logs and test reports.

phases.title: Authenticated live events without unsafe EventSource assumptions
current_code_findings: ControlCenterEventStreamService uses new EventSource(url), which cannot attach Authorization headers.
ControlCenterStateFacade connects to `${base}/api/events/stream` without passing token information.
Backend /api/events/stream uses @check_auth, so the browser auth mechanism must be verified.

implementation_tasks.title: Decide and implement SSE auth strategy
options: Short-lived stream token in query param generated by authenticated POST endpoint
EventSource polyfill/fetch stream with Authorization header support
acceptance_criteria: The chosen strategy is documented in docs/frontend-control-center-endpoint-matrix.md or a dedicated live-events doc.
A logged-in Angular user can connect to /api/events/stream in the browser without dropping auth.
An unauthenticated browser cannot read the event stream.
Tokens are not long-lived broad API tokens placed permanently in URLs.
Playwright/E2E test covers successful connection and unauthorized rejection where practical.

implementation_tasks.title: Filter event stream by user/project/session
implementation_tasks.description: The current stream loops over all tasks and policy decisions. Add filters and authorization checks.
acceptance_criteria: Event stream supports project_id and/or session_id filters where appropriate.
Backend verifies the authenticated user can see the requested project/session stream.
Events from unrelated projects/users are not emitted to the client.
Tests cover at least one negative access case.

implementation_tasks.title: Replace full-scan polling with event source abstraction
implementation_tasks.description: Keep polling only as fallback. Introduce an internal event bus or persisted event log abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: control_center_api.py does not directly call task_repo.get_all() and policy_decision_repo.get_all() forever for every connected client in the final implementation.
Task/session/policy/tool/artifact changes emit ControlCenterEvent objects through a shared service.
SSE can resume from last event id or at least avoid duplicate stale events per connection.
Heartbeat remains present for connection health.

implementation_tasks.title: Fix heartbeat timestamp semantics in Angular
implementation_tasks.description: ControlCenterEventStreamService currently updates lastHeartbeatAt on message events, but SSE heartbeat comments do not trigger onmessage in native EventSource.
acceptance_criteria: Angular correctly distinguishes last data event from last heartbeat/connection health.
If native EventSource cannot observe comment heartbeats, backend emits typed heartbeat data events or Angular uses connection state only.
UI label does not claim 'Letztes Event' when it actually means heartbeat or vice versa.

phases.title: CodeCompass context scope UI and backend binding
current_code_findings: control-center.routes.ts routes /control-center/codecompass to ControlCenterPlaceholderComponent.
control_center_api.py currently returns hardcoded context scopes repo_all, source_only and safe_local.
preview_context_scope() currently returns include/exclude and a simple sensitive path list, not real CodeCompass/RAG graph preview.

implementation_tasks.title: Replace CodeCompass placeholder with real component
implementation_tasks.description: Build a basic context scope selection UI for source paths, docs, tests and graph nodes.
acceptance_criteria: /control-center/codecompass no longer renders only ControlCenterPlaceholderComponent.
UI lists available context scopes from backend.
UI allows selecting include/exclude paths and shows denied secret paths.
UI can attach a selected context scope to a task/session draft.

implementation_tasks.title: Make context scope preview real
implementation_tasks.description: Preview should estimate what would be sent to the worker/model and what is excluded by policy.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/codecompass/context-scopes/preview returns included files/nodes count, excluded files/nodes count and sensitive exclusions.
Preview distinguishes local-only safe context from cloud-eligible context.
Preview never returns raw secret values.
Preview response includes warnings when include patterns are too broad, such as '/'.
Tests cover that /.env, /secrets/**, *.pem and *.key are excluded by default in safe scopes.

implementation_tasks.title: Bind context scope to AgentSession creation
implementation_tasks.description: POST /api/tasks/{taskId}/sessions should accept or derive a context_scope_id and persist it on AgentSessionDB.
acceptance_criteria: AgentSessionDB stores context_scope_id or a frozen context snapshot id.
Session detail response returns the selected context scope/snapshot.
Security Inspector displays context include/exclude summary.
Cloud model/session creation is denied or requires approval when context scope is not cloud-safe.

phases.title: Angular state cleanup, real contracts and memory safety
current_code_findings: ControlCenterStateFacade uses BehaviorSubjects directly and subscribes to stream.lastEventObject$ inside connectEvents without explicit unsubscribe management.
ControlCenterArtifactBrowserComponent subscribes in ngOnInit without cleanup and calls loadContent from a getter, which can trigger repeated calls during change detection.
Several components subscribe manually without takeUntil/destroy handling.

implementation_tasks.title: Avoid API calls from getters
implementation_tasks.description: ControlCenterArtifactBrowserComponent.selected currently calls loadContent(item.id) when item.content is empty. Move this into explicit select/load lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria: No component getter triggers HTTP calls.
Selecting an artifact loads content once unless reload is requested.
Repeated Angular change detection does not repeatedly call artifact content endpoint.
Unit test or component test verifies content is not loaded more than once for same selection.

implementation_tasks.title: Use takeUntilDestroyed or equivalent cleanup
implementation_tasks.description: Clean up subscriptions in components and services that subscribe manually.
acceptance_criteria: Components using manual subscribe also use takeUntilDestroyed, DestroyRef, async pipe or an equivalent cleanup pattern.
ControlCenterStateFacade does not create duplicate event subscriptions when connectEvents is called multiple times.
Navigating away from /control-center/sessions closes event connections and avoids duplicate reconnect loops.

implementation_tasks.title: Make API client contracts match backend envelopes consistently
implementation_tasks.description: Some endpoints use /api prefix while artifacts currently use /artifacts. Normalize or document intentionally.
acceptance_criteria: docs/frontend-control-center-endpoint-matrix.md lists every Angular API call and its backend route.
HubControlCenterApiClient return types match api_response envelope unwrapping behavior of HubApiCoreService.
Artifact endpoints are either exposed under /api/artifacts aliases or documented as legacy /artifacts endpoints.
Contract tests cover the exact shapes consumed by Angular, not only status codes.

phases.title: Testing hardening and CI confidence
current_code_findings: tests/test_control_center_api_contracts.py checks endpoint existence and basic fields but does not yet assert full semantics such as persistence after restart, unauthorized access, real policy binding or artifact relation filtering.
No GitHub combined status was found for the analyzed head, so CI signal is unclear.

implementation_tasks.title: Expand backend API contract tests
acceptance_criteria: Test proves AgentSession task relation persists through repository reload or equivalent persistence boundary.
Test proves wildcard approval is rejected and unknown pending action cannot be approved.
Test proves artifact filters by task_id/session_id exclude unrelated artifacts.
Test proves unauthenticated access to Control Center endpoints is rejected.
Test proves context preview excludes known secret patterns by default.

implementation_tasks.title: Expand Angular component tests
acceptance_criteria: Security Inspector test covers cloudAllowed=true with sensitive allowed path -> warning.
Security Inspector test covers sensitive denied path only -> no cloud warning, but blocked secret shown.
Policy Approval test uses backend-provided pending action row instead of typed ids.
Artifact Browser test verifies filter params are sent to API.
Sessions test verifies worker/model/runtime/policy values are rendered from backend data.

implementation_tasks.title: Add or verify CI checks
acceptance_criteria: CI runs backend tests relevant to Control Center API.
CI runs Angular unit tests for Control Center components/services.
CI runs Playwright smoke tests for key Control Center flows when environment supports it.
CI status is visible for commits or documented if intentionally not enabled.

implementation_tasks.title: Document Control Center architecture
acceptance_criteria: A doc explains the difference between AgentSession, ShareSession/WebRTC session, Task and Artifact.
A Mermaid diagram shows Task -> AgentSession -> ToolCall -> PolicyDecision -> Artifact -> Verification.
A Mermaid diagram shows Angular -> Hub API -> Policy/Worker/Artifact/Event services.
Docs explicitly state that Angular is not the security authority.

implementation_tasks.title: Document endpoint matrix with real status
acceptance_criteria: Every endpoint used by HubControlCenterApiClient is listed with method, path, auth requirement and response shape.
Endpoints still using placeholder/mock/default state are marked as MVP/adapter.
Endpoints that are authoritative for security decisions are marked as backend-enforced.

global_acceptance_criteria: Control Center can display real persisted AgentSessions related to tasks.
Control Center can display real worker id, worker type, model and runtime from backend state.
Control Center can display real ToolCalls for a session.
Control Center can display real PolicySnapshot data including allowed tools, denied tools, allowed paths, denied paths, risk level and cloud/runtime boundary.
Manual approval is possible only for a concrete pending action/tool call and is persisted/audited.
Artifact relationships to task/session/project exist and can be filtered server-side.
Task review surfaces artifact evidence and verification state instead of relying on chat/session text.
SSE or live event transport is authenticated and does not leak cross-project/cross-user events.
Angular no longer presents placeholder worker/model/policy/tool-call values as if they were real.
Security-related UI warnings distinguish allowed-sensitive risk from denied-sensitive safety.
Tests cover both happy paths and security-relevant negative paths.

recommended_commit_plan.commit: backend: add persistent agent sessions for control center
backend: persist tool calls and narrow approvals
backend: expose real policy snapshots to control center
frontend: bind sessions to real backend state
backend: add artifact relations and filters
frontend: make artifact browser API-filtered and review-focused
frontend: add real CodeCompass context scope panel
docs: document control center architecture and contracts

quick_fixes_before_large_refactor.title: Mark placeholder session values visually
quick_fixes_before_large_refactor.description: Until real backend values exist, Angular should display unknown/placeholder labels so the operator does not trust fake policy/model values.
acceptance_criteria: Hardcoded model='n/a' is displayed as unknown/unavailable, not as a real model choice.
Hardcoded allowedPaths=['/'] is removed or clearly marked as placeholder.
Policy inspector shows 'Policy data not yet bound' when backend did not provide policySnapshot.

quick_fixes_before_large_refactor.title: Fix Security Inspector cloud warning logic
acceptance_criteria: Denied secret paths do not trigger cloud risk warning by themselves.
Allowed secret paths trigger warning only when cloudAllowed/runtimeBoundary makes it relevant.
Unit tests cover both cases.

quick_fixes_before_large_refactor.title: Remove HTTP call from ArtifactBrowser getter
acceptance_criteria: selected getter becomes pure and has no side effects.
select(id) performs loading if needed.
No repeated content loads during change detection.

risk_notes: The current MVP API is useful, but using in-memory _TASK_SESSION_LINKS and _APPROVAL_EVENTS for security-relevant workflow can mislead future implementation if not replaced soon.
The current Angular Control Center looks more complete than its backend truth currently is; placeholder state must be clearly marked or removed.
Native EventSource auth must be solved before relying on live events in a secured deployment.
Artifact rendering security is on the right path with DOMPurify, but Mermaid rendering should remain sandboxed/sanitized when real rendering is introduced.

execution_progress.first_batch_summary: First 10 tasks started and implemented as done/partial across backend persistence and frontend quick-fixes.
execution_progress.second_batch_summary: P2 core started: persisted ToolCalls, validation-backed approvals, and frontend pending-action approval selection.

items.note: Placeholder values are now shown as unknown/unavailable in sessions and policy snapshot marked as placeholder.
Security inspector cloud warning now triggers only for sensitive allowed paths when cloud is allowed.
Artifact browser getter is pure; loading moved to explicit select/load lifecycle.
Added persistent AgentSessionDB model and repository wiring.
Removed in-memory task-session mapping usage from control_center_api and switched to AgentSession persistence.
Introduced normalized AgentSession status lifecycle handling in backend.
Removed HTTP side effect from selected getter in artifact browser.
Added subscription cleanup in artifact browser; broader facade/component cleanup still pending.
Extended policy model with cloud/runtime boundary and updated security inspector rendering/warning logic; tests still pending.
Sessions now map worker/model/runtime from backend when present, with unknown fallback.
Added persistent ToolCallDB, repository and session tool-call endpoint; full secrecy/redaction model still pending.
Approval now validates ownership + pending action and updates auditable ToolCall state (allowed + approved_by_user_id) without in-memory approval cache.
Angular approval component now selects real pending rows (require_approval) instead of free-text ids.
Added persistent PolicySnapshotDB and bound it to AgentSession via policy_snapshot_id; session responses include real policy snapshot fields.
Sessions view now maps real worker/model/runtime and full backend session status lifecycle instead of hardcoded defaults.
Added API-side artifact filters (project_id/task_id/session_id/type/limit/offset) and Angular now sends filter params to backend.
Extended backend API contract tests with approval negative cases and artifact filter semantics.
